summary: Kazuo Ishiguro, the British author of The Remains of the Day, won the Nobel Prize for Literature on Thursday for a run of exquisite novels that the Swedish Academy said mixed Franz Kafka with Jane Austen.

title: Are you an Alaskan? Check your bank account for an extra $1,100 today
summary: The state of Alaska began distributing its annual Permanent Fund dividend checks Thursday. This year's payment, which comes from a pool of investments originally purchased with oil revenue, is $1,100.

summary: Troopers said one person was thrown from a car and killed and several others were injured when two cars crashed near Mile 53.

title: Anchorage Assembly will hold unusual Saturday hearing on SB 91
summary: Assembly Chair Dick Traini said he couldn’t recall the Assembly holding a public hearing on a resolution that relates to state legislation.

summary: The prospect is located near salmon-producing Bristol Bay headwaters, and the company has faced fierce opposition from critics who fear it will devastate the region's valuable salmon fishery.

title: Interior Alaska man dies in suspected carbon monoxide poisoning

title: Alaska Gov. Walker’s administration convenes climate change meeting as it prepares release of new policy
summary: Dozens of stakeholders on Wednesday participated in an unusual public discussion about climate change — an effort that’s expected to inform a forthcoming state climate change action plan.

title: Voters decisively reject cannabis industry bans in local Alaska elections
summary: Measures to shut down all cannabis businesses failed Tuesday in the city of Fairbanks, the Fairbanks North Star Borough and the Kenai Peninsula Borough.

title: Police identify Seward officer – at first mistakenly – involved in fatal shooting during traffic stop
summary: Neither Seward police nor the Alaska State Troopers would offer any additional information on the shooting of Micah McComas of Chugiak on Sunday.

summary: In the Mat-Su borough, incumbent Assembly member Dan Mayfield narrowly trailed a conservative challenger, Mokie Tew, while mayoral elections in the Kenai and North Slope boroughs appeared to be heading to runoffs.

title: Bristol Bay region braces for long-awaited Pebble mine plan
summary: On Thursday, Pebble CEO Tom Collier is expected to roll out details of the company's new, smaller plan to mine the copper and gold deposit northwest of Iliamna.

title: Yahoo says all 3 billion accounts hacked in 2013 data theft
summary: Yahoo on Tuesday said that all 3 billion of its accounts were hacked in a 2013 data theft, tripling its earlier estimate of the size of the largest breach in history.

title: Judge voids order from Alaska oil well regulators for violating bankruptcy law
summary: A federal bankruptcy judge in Alaska voided an order from a state regulatory agency, leaving in question the sale of a Cook Inlet gas field.

title: Alaska cannabis revenue climbs again in August
summary: Alaska's commercial marijuana growers have paid more than $3 million in taxes to the state since sales began in October 2016.

title: Anchorage police plan new crime suppression strategy
summary: Police plan to create a new investigations unit, dedicate a detective to data analysis and restructure the divisions that conduct drug busts in hopes of better combating crime.

summary: Alaska Airlines aims to increase its cargo hauling capacity with new aircraft.

title: Investigators find arsenal fit for a commando team in Las Vegas hotel suite

title: Federal watchdog opens probe into travel by Interior Secretary Ryan Zinke

summary: After studying the satellite imagery of the area, I identified a 100-mile ridge that looked bikeable. Of course, most things look bikeable from outer space.

summary: Prosecutors say Michael Chalender used the heroin he got from Hiram Ducasse on Nov. 30, 2015. By the next morning, he was dead of a drug overdose.

title: Las Vegas shooter’s father was a bank robber and on the FBI’s Most Wanted list
summary: His father had been convicted of bank robbery, auto theft and a confidence scam when he escaped from prison Feb. 3, 1969, in El Paso, Texas. At the time, his son Stephen would have been 15 years old.

summary: Micah McComas, 41, was shot and later died of his injuries, troopers said Monday. An officer, who hasn’t yet been named, was injured.

summary: John Sturgeon hasn't been allowed to use his hovercraft in an Alaska preserve since 2007.

title: Alaska Native activist Desa Jacobsson, known for attention-grabbing protests, is dead at 69
summary: Jacobsson’s confrontational style, which included weekslong public hunger strikes and other acts of protest, was designed to “bring attention to those who didn’t have a voice,” her daughter said.

title: New effort underway to find how much oil is beneath ANWR, NPR-A
summary: Interior Secretary Ryan Zinke has called for new reviews of the oil and gas potential in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge and the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska.

title: Trump says he’ll look into Price’s private-jet use
summary: President Trump said he was “not happy” with Tom Price, his secretary of Health and Human Services, who has chartered at least $400,000 in private jet travel.

title: A North Korean ship was seized off Egypt with a huge cache of weapons destined for a surprising buyer
summary: Last August, a secret message was passed from Washington to Cairo warning about a mysterious vessel steaming toward the Suez Canal.

title: U.S. ‘body clock’ scientists win Nobel medicine prize

title: Flu season hits hard and early in northern Alaska
summary: With more than 70 confirmed cases in September, which was more than any other part of the state, the northern region was of interest to state health officials.

title: Twitter finds hundreds of accounts tied to Russian operatives
summary: Twitter has shut down 201 accounts that were tied to the same Russian operatives who posted thousands of political ads on Facebook, the company told congressional investigators Thursday and revealed in a blog in the afternoon.

title: Preparing for the worst nature can offer
summary: A recent cascade of disasters has reminded many Americans that there is no time like the present to think about all the things that could go wrong.
